rulings.Case Title: ERIK MCKEE ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE KEAYS ET AL VS. 50 BEALE INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendants Paramount Group, Inc.'s and 50 Beale Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is sustained on the ground that the complaint does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action and because, subsequent to this Court's ruling on D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s demurrer, plaintiffs have represented that they plan to amend the complaint to address the arguments made by defendants here. It is sustained with leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient (1) to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71 by identifying the product(s) for which defendants are allegedly responsible, and (2) to identify the premise(s) at issue for which plaintiffs allege defendants, identified as Premises Defendants, are responsible. Pursuant to this Court's ruling on Zelinsky's demurrer and the declaration of Nectaria Belantis, plaintiffs' amended complaint remains due by July 9, 2015. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: VELMA MCALLISTER VS. FOSTER WHEELER LLC(FKA FOSTER WHEELER CORPORATION) ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 8, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc.'s special demurrer to plaintiff's fourth cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's fourth cause of action alleges sufficient facts to meet the standards set forth in Campbell v. Ford Motor Co., (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = 503/TLJ)
